name: Wolters Kluwer CCH Tax Software API
what_it_does: Wolters Kluwer CCH Axcess and ProSystem fx tax software REST API for federal, state, and international tax return preparation and compliance. Enables AI agents to manage tax return data import and populating tax forms for corporate and individual tax return automation, handle tax workpaper preparation and source document extraction for tax workflow automation, access entity tax data and multi-entity consolidation for corporate tax compliance, retrieve state and local tax (SALT) data for multi-jurisdiction tax compliance, manage tax provision and ASC 740 calculation for financial reporting tax automation, handle international tax calculation (GILTI, FDII, BEAT) for multinational corporation compliance, access estimated tax payment scheduling and management for tax cash flow automation, retrieve tax return status and due date management for deadline tracking automation, manage e-file transmission and acknowledgment tracking for electronic filing, and integrate CCH with ERP, GL, and tax provision platforms.
use_cases: Populating tax returns from accounting data agents
Managing multi-entity tax from corporate compliance agents
Calculating tax provision from ASC 740 agents
Tracking e-file status from tax operations agents
not_for: Consumer personal tax preparation (use TurboTax or H&R Block)
Tax research and law (use CCH IntelliConnect or Checkpoint)
best_when: A large accounting firm, corporate tax department, or tax technology team wanting AI agents to automate federal and state tax return data population, entity management, tax provision calculation, e-filing workflow, and multi-jurisdiction compliance.
avoid_when: TAX RETURN ACCURACY AND CPA RESPONSIBILITY: Automated tax return data population must be reviewed and approved by CPA or EA before filing; automated tax return filing without professional review creates practitioner liability under Treasury Circular 230 and state CPA licensing rules. TAX ACCURACY FOR SIGNIFICANT TAX POSITIONS: Automated tax position calculation for complex areas (GILTI, transfer pricing, R&D credits) must be validated by qualified tax professional; automated tax with incorrect positions creates IRS audit and tax understatement penalty exposure. Estimated tax underpayment — automated estimated tax payment scheduling must correctly calculate quarterly payment amounts to avoid underpayment penalty; automated underpayment creates IRS/state penalty and interest. State nexus and apportionment accuracy — automated multi-state tax apportionment must apply each state's specific apportionment formula (single-sales factor, three-factor, etc.); automated incorrect apportionment creates state audit and assessment risk.

auth.notes: Wolters Kluwer CCH uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Alphen aan den Rijn, Netherlands HQ (Wolters Kluwer, AEX: WKL). Founded 1836 (Wolters Kluwer); CCH founded 1913. Public. €5.5B+ annual revenue. CCH Axcess (cloud) and ProSystem fx (on-premise). 200,000+ tax and accounting professional users. Competes with Thomson Reuters Checkpoint, Drake Software, and Intuit ProConnect for professional tax software.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-return or per-user professional subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; per-return, per-user, and enterprise firm pricing
pricing.notes: Netherlands HQ (AEX:WKL). €5.5B+ revenue. CCH Axcess cloud and ProSystem fx. 200,000+ professional users. Per-return and per-user professional pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; e-file transmission limits per IRS/state MeF guidelines; documented in CCH developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: CPA REVIEW REQUIRED BEFORE E-FILE: Automated tax return data population and e-file transmission must include CPA or EA review and sign-off; automated e-file without professional preparer review violates Treasury Circular 230 and creates practitioner liability; implement mandatory human approval gate before e-file transmission

No webhooks — e-file acknowledgment and IRS/state return processing status require polling; IRS MeF acknowledgment can take minutes to hours; implement scheduled polling for e-file status with appropriate interval

State tax law annual change risk — automated multi-state tax return population must apply current-year state tax law changes; CCH tax rules update seasonally; automated workflows using prior-year rules create state assessment and penalty risk

GILTI and international tax complexity — automated GILTI, FDII, and BEAT calculation for multinational corporations involves complex tested income and qualified business asset investment (QBAI) calculations; automated international tax position without specialist review creates substantial understatement penalty exposure

CCH Axcess vs ProSystem fx API differences — CCH Axcess (cloud) and ProSystem fx (on-premise) have different API architectures; firm using on-premise ProSystem fx requires different integration than cloud Axcess; confirm product deployment before building

Estimated tax payment accuracy — automated estimated tax payment scheduling for corporate taxpayers must correctly calculate safe harbor amount (100% of prior year or 100% of current year); automated underpayment below safe harbor creates IRS penalty and interest
agent_readiness.security_notes: Tax software. SOC2, IRS efile certified, GDPR. OAuth2 with scopes. US/EU. Taxpayer financial and tax return data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Professional tax software. SOC2, IRS efile, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Tax return and financial data.
